office_does: The prosecutor leads felony and juvenile prosecution, represents the county in civil matters, advises county government, and sets charging and diversion priorities within state law.
race_blurb: Both candidates are deputy prosecutors. Danny Tarkenton emphasizes psychology-informed recidivism reduction and political independence, while incumbent Preston McCollam emphasizes enforcement, victim advocacy, and institutional continuity.

candidates.summary: A deputy prosecutor and Iraq War veteran, Tarkenton emphasizes behavioral evidence, recidivism reduction, and independence from party organizations.
highlights: Handles criminal and involuntary-treatment proceedings and reports a docket exceeding 500 cases [S1]
Says psychology-informed prosecution can reduce repeat offending [S1][S2]
Frames independence from political parties as important to prosecutorial discretion [S1]

safety.basis: Her public-safety approach emphasizes reducing recidivism through psychology-informed prosecution.

experience.basis: She has substantial current prosecutorial responsibility but not the incumbent's senior management record.

reform.basis: She explicitly argues prosecutorial discretion should be independent of political parties.

candidates.summary: The incumbent prosecutor, McCollam emphasizes conventional enforcement, crime-victim advocacy, and continuity in senior office management.
highlights: Has served as county prosecutor since July 2025 after more than a decade in criminal and civil prosecution [S1]
Promises law-enforcement collaboration, offender accountability, and victim advocacy [S1][S2]
Previously served as chief criminal deputy and interim civil chief [S1]

safety.basis: He repeatedly centers law-enforcement collaboration, offender accountability, and conventional prosecution.

experience.basis: He is the incumbent with more than a decade of prosecution and senior office-management experience.

reform.basis: His candidacy emphasizes institutional continuity and endorsements from prosecutors and law-enforcement officials.
